source_policy: Brain owns stable product semantics. Desktop manifests describe installed UI surfaces. Backend endpoints own live state, prices, balances, tickets and provisioning.

capabilities.title: Product Self-Knowledge
capabilities.summary: NEXO can answer what its own runtime, tools, scripts, projects and supported workflows are by using structured local catalog sources.

live_state.fallback: Answer only from this catalog and source_refs; do not guess unsupported capabilities.

read: Explain a tool or product capability

safety.credential_policy: Do not expose credentials or secret values.
safety.retention: No new user data is stored by read-only lookup.
safety.audit: Tool calls are covered by the standard NEXO session/tool logs.
forbidden_actions: Invent unsupported product features
Treat static docs as live backend state

must_say: Use the live catalog first when available.
Mark uncertain or missing capabilities as unknown.
must_not_say: Do not claim runtime parity that is not in the catalog.

capabilities.title: Desktop Preferences And Managed Settings
capabilities.summary: NEXO Desktop exposes non-technical settings through the Settings UI and a structured preference catalog.

live_state.source: Desktop app settings + Brain calibration/profile files
live_state.fallback: Tell the user the setting exists only if present in the generated Desktop manifest.

write: Set one supported preference through settings-preference-set

safety.data_origin: local user profile and Desktop app state
safety.consent_required: yes before changing preferences that affect behavior
safety.confirmation_required: yes for destructive, account, billing, permission or automation changes
safety.credential_policy: Never ask for passwords in chat; use Desktop connection flows or credential tools.
safety.retention: Settings persist in local app settings and Brain profile/calibration files.
safety.audit: Preference changes must pass through settings-save/settings-preference-set and standard logs.
forbidden_actions: Apply side effects from raw UI onChange
Bypass Desktop Settings for normal users

must_say: Guide non-technical users through Desktop Settings first.
must_not_say: Do not tell users to edit JSON files for normal preference changes.

capabilities.title: Local Memory And Local File Index
capabilities.summary: NEXO can index approved local folders for local retrieval while preserving exclusions, retention limits and diagnostics.

live_state.source: local index service status and indexed roots
live_state.fallback: If status is unavailable, describe only the configured design and ask to inspect diagnostics.

safety.consent_required: yes before adding roots or clearing indexed data
safety.confirmation_required: yes for clear-index, purge, broad roots and sensitive folders
safety.credential_policy: Do not index credential files; keep secret patterns excluded.
safety.retention: Must follow local retention and disk growth limits.
safety.audit: Root/exclusion changes and purge operations must be logged.
forbidden_actions: Index home directory broadly without review
Ignore exclusion rules
Claim cloud upload for local-only index

must_say: Local memory is local-first and controlled from Desktop settings.
must_not_say: Do not imply all user files are indexed by default.

capabilities.title: Managed Agent Email
capabilities.summary: NEXO can use a managed IMAP/SMTP agent mailbox when the account exists, is configured and the user has enabled email automation.

live_state.source: Desktop settings + backend NEXO Email API + local automation supervisor
live_state.fallback: If not configured, explain the setup route without inventing credentials.

write: Send email only with explicit authorization and account readiness

safety.data_origin: managed mailbox and local email monitor
safety.consent_required: yes before enabling monitoring or sending email
safety.confirmation_required: yes before sending external email or changing account settings
safety.credential_policy: Never request or reveal mailbox passwords in chat; use managed account connection flows.
safety.retention: Email processing follows local logs and mailbox retention; avoid storing full message bodies unnecessarily.
safety.audit: Sent email and monitor actions require verifiable logs.
forbidden_actions: Send email without current user authorization
Use private followups as support tickets
Expose SMTP/IMAP secrets

must_say: Email works only when the mailbox is configured and enabled.
must_not_say: Do not promise sending if readiness was not checked.

capabilities.title: Product Automations
capabilities.summary: NEXO ships managed automations for email monitoring, followups and morning briefings; Desktop exposes safe controls for product automations.

live_state.fallback: Describe packaged automation categories, not individual running status.

write: Change supported cadence or extra instructions

safety.consent_required: yes before enabling/disabling or changing cadence
safety.confirmation_required: yes for changes that send email, notify users or alter recurring work
safety.credential_policy: Automations must use configured managed credentials; no secrets in chat.
safety.retention: Automation logs follow runtime log retention.
safety.audit: Automation changes and runs must be traceable.
forbidden_actions: Expose technical RSS/feed setup to normal users for Morning Digest
Claim an automation is running without checking health

must_say: Morning Digest is a managed briefing automation, not a raw feed configuration surface.
must_not_say: Do not say there are only three automations if the Desktop manifest reports four product automation identifiers.

capabilities.title: Support Tickets
capabilities.summary: Product bugs, setup failures and customer support reports belong in the backend support ticket system.

live_state.fallback: Create a local draft only if backend is unavailable; do not treat reminders as real tickets.

safety.consent_required: yes before sending diagnostics or personal data to support
safety.confirmation_required: yes before creating external support tickets with attachments
safety.credential_policy: Use authenticated Desktop/backend session; do not ask for backend tokens in chat.
safety.retention: Backend support retention applies; diagnostic bundles must be minimized and redacted.
safety.audit: Ticket IDs and API results are the evidence of support actions.
forbidden_actions: Use private reminders/followups instead of support tickets
Attach logs without permission

must_say: Real product reports should be filed as support tickets.
must_not_say: Do not say a ticket was created without ticket ID evidence.

capabilities.title: NEXO Credits Provider Proxy
capabilities.summary: NEXO Credits routes billable provider calls through backend policy, estimation, reservation and reconciliation.

live_state.fallback: Mark provider/model support as unknown or planned if not returned by backend.

write: Execute provider call after explicit user intent

safety.consent_required: yes before spending credits or sending payloads to providers
safety.confirmation_required: yes before billable provider calls or top-ups
safety.credential_policy: Provider credentials live in backend credential storage; never reveal them.
safety.retention: Provider request records and ledger retention follow backend policy.
safety.audit: Credit reservation, ledger and provider request IDs are required evidence.
forbidden_actions: Hardcode prices or balances
Promise video/model invocation unless backend marks it invokable
Spend credits silently

must_say: Balances, prices and invokable models are live backend facts.
must_not_say: Do not infer provider availability from marketing copy.

capabilities.title: Managed Cloud And Edge
capabilities.summary: NEXO can list and provision managed cloud/edge resources through NEXO Credits when backend routes and spend controls are available.

live_state.fallback: Listing can be described as safe discovery; provisioning remains billable and must not be promised without route confirmation.

safety.data_origin: backend managed infrastructure and third-party cloud APIs
safety.consent_required: yes before provisioning or changing infrastructure
safety.confirmation_required: yes before billable, public or DNS-changing operations
safety.credential_policy: Cloud provider credentials are backend-managed; do not expose tokens.
safety.retention: Backend resource and job retention applies.
safety.audit: Provisioning/job IDs and credit ledger entries are required evidence.
forbidden_actions: Register domains without explicit approval
Change DNS silently
State a server/route/IP without verification

must_say: Listing existing managed infrastructure is safe; creation is billable and approval-gated.
must_not_say: Do not claim a domain/server exists without live verification.

capabilities.title: Protocol Cards
capabilities.summary: Official workflow cards are served by backend and matched by Brain when available, so product procedures can evolve without hardcoded prompts.

live_state.fallback: If unauthenticated/unavailable, continue with local product docs and say nothing about internal failure unless operationally relevant.

write: Activate card when backend supports it and user context requires it

safety.confirmation_required: depends on actions inside the matched card
safety.credential_policy: Use existing authenticated Desktop/backend session; do not expose tokens.
safety.audit: Card match/use is captured by runtime task evidence when relevant.
forbidden_actions: Invent official protocols
Expose internal card fetch failures as user-facing noise

must_say: Follow official cards silently when available.
must_not_say: Do not present backend-auth failures as product limitations.
